title: Data Center POWER eXchange
content:
Data Center POWER eXchange, a conference focused on power generation and digital infrastructure, will convene utilities, data center operators, developers, policymakers and investors in Washington, D.C., on September 30 to October 1, 2026.

The event, held at the Omni Shoreham hotel, addresses power challenges as artificial intelligence and digital infrastructure drive unprecedented electricity demand. Organizers said the conference brings together industry stakeholders to align power systems with rapid data center expansion across grid interconnection, transmission capacity, on-site generation and reliability strategies.

The conference is created by POWER Magazine, a publication covering the power generation industry for more than a century. Washington, D.C. hosts the event at the intersection of energy policy, infrastructure planning and one of the fastest-growing data center regions in the United States.

The program is structured around three themes: grid capacity and interconnection challenges, generation and energy mix strategies, and scaling infrastructure to meet accelerating demand. Sessions address how the industry can move from identifying constraints to executing solutions across the power and data center sectors.

Data Center POWER eXchange held its inaugural event in 2025. The conference organizers said attendees can register on the event website, with options available for exhibitors and sponsors seeking to participate.

Source: https://www.datacenterpowerexchange.com/
source_url: https://www.datacenterpowerexchange.com/ "Data Center POWER eXchange"

title: Saudi Arabia Innovates to Support Renewable Energy Goals
content:
Saudi Arabia is pursuing advanced thermal management solutions for high-efficiency solar cells as part of its broader renewable energy expansion strategy outlined in Vision 2030.

The kingdom aims to generate 50% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030, with plans to develop 130 GW of renewable energy capacity. This target includes approximately 58.7 GW from solar energy and 40 GW from wind energy.

Triple-junction photovoltaic cells, which offer superior energy conversion capabilities, face significant operational challenges in high-temperature environments, including thermal degradation and non-uniform heat distribution. Effective thermal management is critical to maintaining efficiency and system longevity.

Active cooling systems integrated into photovoltaic installations can enhance performance in Saudi Arabia's arid climate, where high ambient temperatures reduce solar output. By mitigating temperature-related efficiency losses, active cooling allows solar installations to operate closer to their optimal levels.

The adoption of advanced cooling technologies aligns with Vision 2030's objectives of reducing oil dependence, diversifying the economy, and fostering innovation. Saudi Arabia's vast solar irradiance potential makes the durability and efficiency of advanced photovoltaic systems particularly important for the country's energy transition.

Integrating cooling solutions into solar infrastructure supports the development of a knowledge-based economy while positioning Saudi Arabia as a leader in renewable energy technologies.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-arabia-innovates-to-support-renewable-energy-goals/

title: Carbon Capture Projects Come Online in Iceland, Saudi Arabia
content:
Two carbon capture installations began operations in Iceland and Saudi Arabia, advancing direct capture and storage technologies in different regions.

Officials in Iceland inaugurated Steingerdur, a carbon capture facility integrated with ON Power's geothermal plant, transforming it into one of the world's first near-zero emission geothermal power stations. The installation uses direct water capture to remove carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide emissions from the geothermal steam cycle, then applies Carbfix technology to store the captured minerals underground where they mineralize into rock within approximately two years. Steingerdur is expected to deliver about 10% of Iceland's 2030 Climate Action Plan targets for the energy and industrial sectors.

Iceland also hosts Orca, a direct air capture and storage facility operated by Switzerland-based Climeworks that came online in September 2021.

In Saudi Arabia, Climeworks, the King Abdullah Petroleum Studies and Research Center, and the Saudi Energy Ministry inaugurated a direct air capture testing unit at KAPSARC's facilities in Riyadh. The mobile unit captures carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere and demonstrates Climeworks' technology under hot and arid conditions representative of the region. KAPSARC is a non-profit research institution focused on energy economics and global sustainability that serves as Saudi Arabia's leading energy think tank.

Climeworks opened a U.S. headquarters in Austin, Texas last year. ON Power and Carbfix are both part of Reykjavík Energy.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/carbon-capture-projects-come-online-in-iceland-saudi-arabia/

title: Aramco, Rondo Energy Studying GW-Scale Thermal Storage, Hydrogen, Carbon Capture Deployment
content:
Saudi Aramco and Rondo Energy have signed a memorandum of understanding to conduct engineering studies for a project combining thermal energy storage, hydrogen production and carbon capture at industrial scale, the companies said on May 16.

The joint project will involve the first deployment of Rondo's heat battery technology at Aramco facilities globally to lower operating costs and support the Saudi oil giant's plan to cut carbon emissions. The companies said the project could scale up to 1 GWh, with potential for further expansion across Aramco's worldwide operations.

Aramco and Rondo will explore joint development of novel applications for the Rondo Heat Battery technology, specifically targeting hydrogen production and carbon capture solutions. Aramco Ventures previously invested equity in Rondo Energy in July 2023.

Rondo's heat battery system stores energy from wind and solar power and supplies continuous energy at high temperatures needed for industrial processes at gigawatt scale.

Ali Al-Meshari, Aramco senior vice president of Technology, Oversight and Coordination, said the company aims to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 operational greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 across its wholly owned and operated assets. "Innovative technologies deployed at scale are needed to reduce the costs of emissions reduction," Al-Meshari said.

California-based Rondo Energy develops lower-carbon industrial heat and power solutions. The memorandum of understanding marks a significant step in commercializing thermal energy storage technology at industrial scale.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/aramco-rondo-energy-studying-gw-scale-thermal-storage-hydrogen-carbon-capture-deployment/

title: Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt
content:
A Saudi Arabian energy company signed an agreement with Egyptian authorities on July 17 to develop a 10-GW wind farm west of Sohag in Upper Egypt.

ACWA Power, a Saudi-based water desalination and power company, and Egypt's New and Renewable Energy Authority signed a memorandum of understanding allocating approximately 3,000 square kilometers of land for the project. The facility represents the next phase following an initial agreement signed in November 2022 between ACWA Power, Egypt's Electricity Transmission Company, and the renewable energy authority.

Egypt's Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy Mohamed Shaker Al-Marqabi said the project aligns with the country's target to increase renewable energy capacity to 42% of the energy mix by 2035 and supports Egypt's Vision 2030 framework. He described the development as reflecting broader cooperation between Egypt and Saudi Arabia.

Officials said the wind farm is projected to save Egypt approximately $6.5 billion annually in energy costs. The country is accelerating renewable energy deployment to reduce reliance on natural gas-fired power generation and lower carbon emissions.

ACWA Power operates globally in desalination, power generation and renewable energy sectors. The company's involvement in the Egyptian wind project marks its expansion into utility-scale renewable generation in the Middle East and North Africa region.

The facility's development timeline and financial structure remain to be detailed in subsequent phases of the project.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/ "Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt"

title: China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan
content:
China's Envision Energy will supply 1 GW of wind turbine generation capacity for projects in Uzbekistan designed by Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power, the Shanghai-based manufacturer announced on June 20.

Envision will provide its EN171-6.5 turbines to ACWA's Bash and Dzhankeldy onshore wind farms in Uzbekistan. The deal follows several large turbine contracts Envision secured in recent months in Egypt, India and Saudi Arabia.

The announcement comes after Envision was selected to supply approximately 1.7 GW of turbines to Saudi Arabia's Neom hydrogen facility, valued at $8.4 billion. That project will integrate 4 GW of solar, wind and battery energy storage capacity to produce up to 600 tonnes daily of green ammonia, powered by more than 2 GW of hydrogen electrolysis.

Envision ranks as China's second-largest wind turbine manufacturer and also operates among the country's leading energy management software companies. BloombergNEF ranked Envision the world's fourth-largest turbine supplier last year with 8.3 GW of installed capacity.

Uzbekistan has set a target to install at least 5 GW of onshore wind capacity by 2030 as part of broader renewable energy expansion plans. ACWA Power signed a $10 billion investment cooperation agreement with Uzbekistan's Ministry of Energy in 2023 for renewable energy, green hydrogen and gas-to-power projects over the following five years.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/ "China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan"

title: ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project
content:
ACWA Power inaugurated the 300 MW Sakaka photovoltaic project in Al Jouf, Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy installation, at a cost of 1.2 billion Saudi riyals.

The ceremony took place on April 9, 2021, under the patronage of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz Bin Salman Bin Abdulaziz Al Saud and Al Jouf Governor Prince Faisal bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz attended the event.

The project was awarded to an ACWA Power-led consortium in 2018 at a tariff of 8.781 halalas per kilowatt-hour, described as a world record at the time. The pricing achievement helped enable subsequent projects under Saudi Arabia's National Renewable Energy Program to reduce clean solar electricity costs in the Kingdom.

Prince Abdulaziz Bin Salman Bin Abdulaziz said the private sector played a fundamental role in developing renewable energy projects in Saudi Arabia. He noted that the Public Investment Fund holds 50% ownership of ACWA Power, which operates across thirteen countries. He stated that 97% of the operating team at Sakaka PV are Saudi nationals and 90% of the workforce comes from the Al Jouf region.

The inauguration marks a milestone in Saudi Arabia's energy diversification strategy. ACWA Power develops, invests in and operates power generation and water desalination plants.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/ "ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project"

title: Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia
content:
Doosan Škoda Power has successfully commissioned two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili steam-gas combined cycle power plant near Jubail in Saudi Arabia, with operations beginning in March 2020.

The two DST-S10 condensation turbines without reheating will produce 418 MW of the plant's total 1,509 MW capacity. The Fadhili facility is jointly owned by Saudi Aramco, Saudi Electricity Company and French utility Engie. Of the plant's total output, 1,100 MW will supply electricity to local industry and residents, while 400 MW will serve the power plant's own operations and gas extraction activities.

Doosan Škoda Power supplied not only the turbines but also associated equipment including pipelines, control systems with field equipment, steam compression and drainage systems, oil systems and high-pressure hydraulic systems.

Project manager Jindřich Závodný said the execution proceeded smoothly due to cooperation within the group with EPC supplier Doosan Heavy Industries & Construction. He noted the project required compliance with local standards and seismic codes while operating at a frequency of 60 Hz, different from typical European systems.

Závodný added that installation and commissioning were supervised by experienced technical advisers who worked with Korean colleagues to hand over the units for commercial operation. The units are currently under warranty, with Doosan Škoda Power providing complete support to ensure smooth operation.

This marks Doosan Škoda Power's first installation in Saudi Arabia. The company also operates turbines at other Middle Eastern projects, including facilities in Zarqa.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/

title: Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy
content:
Wärtsilä will supply a 44 MW hybrid power plant combining engine technology and solar energy for the Mansourah & Massarah Gold Project mine in Saudi Arabia, marking the country's first newbuild power project to integrate both systems.

The order was placed in March by Larsen & Toubro Ltd, the India-based main contractors. The plant will comprise six Wärtsilä 32 engines, with five operating and one on constant standby. Wärtsilä's engine technology can reach full power output within minutes of startup, enabling the facility to efficiently balance fluctuating solar supply.

Renewable energy sources like solar and wind require rapid power plant response to manage variable generation. The Wärtsilä system's flexibility addresses this challenge by adjusting output quickly as solar production varies throughout the day.

Amit Swarnkar, MENA BU Head of Metallurgical and Material Handling Division at Larsen & Toubro Ltd, said reliability is essential for uninterrupted mine operations. "We have selected the Wärtsilä technology because of its capability to enable the integration of PV solar into the system, which is important for this project," he commented.

Alexandre Eykerman, Energy Business Director, Middle East at Wärtsilä, added that "maximising the use of renewable energy is central to Wärtsilä's approach to enabling sustainable energy supplies."

Equipment delivery will occur on a fast-track basis within 10 months from contract signing. The plant is scheduled for commissioning by May 2021, with commercial operations beginning a year later.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/

title: ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market
content:
ACWA Power signed an implementation agreement with Azerbaijan's Ministry of Energy to develop, build and operate a 240 MW wind power project, marking the Saudi Arabian company's entry into the country's market.

The power purchase agreement covers a 20-year period on a Build Own Operate basis, the company said during a ceremony in Baku on January 9, 2020, attended by Azerbaijan's Prime Minister Ali Asadov and Energy Minister Parviz Shahbazov.

The project is one of the first two independent power projects in Azerbaijan and supports the government's efforts to diversify its energy mix and reduce dependence on oil and gas revenues. Azerbaijan is undertaking major reforms to strengthen energy security by leveraging the country's renewable energy resources.

Paddy Padmanathan, Chief Executive Officer of ACWA Power, said the agreement "expands our geographic footprint of operations into this culturally rich and economically vibrant country straddling Europe and Asia." He added that the investment "reinforces the pivotal role that ACWA Power is committed to playing in deploying Saudi foreign investment to promote the decarbonisation of the electricity generation sector."

ACWA Power said its expertise in renewable energy project development would help reduce generation costs and provide affordable access to energy for Azerbaijan's communities.

Rajit Nanda, Chief Investment Officer of ACWA Power, attended the signing ceremony alongside Mohammad Abunayyan, the company's Chairman.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/ "ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market"

title: Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East
content:
Solar power projects across the Middle East and North Africa are expected to attract at least $15 billion in investment over the next five years, according to the Middle East Solar Industry Association.

The region's solar market is valued at more than $20 billion, with photovoltaic technology now representing the most competitive form of power generation in the Gulf region, according to the International Renewable Energy Agency. Gulf Cooperation Council countries, including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, plan to install nearly 7 GW of new renewable energy capacity by the early 2020s.

Saudi Arabia has advanced to a second phase of its solar procurement plan, opening bidding for seven projects totaling more than 1.5 GW of capacity. Oman has begun procuring at least 1 GW of residential solar generation, while Algeria announced tenders for 2 GW of solar capacity.

The UAE leads the region with the largest operational and in-the-pipeline solar projects, accounting for 58% of the MENA region's total, followed by Egypt at 23%, Jordan at 10%, and Morocco at 6%, with Iran representing 3%, according to Mina Mesbahi, a research analyst at Solarplaza International B.V. The region currently operates almost 3 GW of solar capacity.

"Almost all the countries in this region experience all year-round sunshine, providing breeding grounds for solar development," Mesbahi said.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/ "Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East"

title: NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY
content:
Nationwide Boiler Inc. has secured its largest rental project in 51 years, supplying three 200,000 lb/hr watertube boilers to Saudi Aramco's Jizan Refinery in Saudi Arabia.

The Fremont, California-based company, a distributor of Babcock & Wilcox package boilers, will provide the units at 750 psi design and 750F superheated steam specifications for a minimum one-year rental during the refinery's commissioning phase. The equipment package also includes a plant master workstation and one deaerator tank with pumps.

The boilers were sourced from Nationwide Boiler's existing inventory, allowing for rapid deployment. "Having three high capacity watertube boilers complete with immediate availability was key in securing the order," said William Testa, the company's Director of Sales. "But a significant amount of preparation, multiple discussions to formulate an offering that fit their needs best, and a committed team with all hands-on deck is really what sealed the deal."

Nationwide Boiler worked with its new Middle East representative, Aujan Industrial Solutions, and EPC contractor Redland Industrial Services (Arabia) Ltd. to secure the contract.

The Jizan facility spans 106 square kilometers and is expected to process more than 400,000 barrels per day of crude oil for domestic and international markets. The refinery will supply power to locally owned manufacturing and service companies.

The equipment will ship via roll-on-roll-off vessel from Houston, with an expected transit time of six weeks to the Port of Jizan.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/ "NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY"

title: ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia
content:
ACWA Power has won the contract for the Skaka photovoltaic solar project, the first utility-scale renewable energy plant in Saudi Arabia, at a tariff of 8.782 halala/kWh, equivalent to 2.34 U.S. cents/kWh.

The 300 MW plant will be located in Al Jouf on a site spanning more than six square kilometers. The project carries an investment value of SR 993,750 million, or US $256 million, with a 25-year power purchase contract.

The Skaka project represents the opening phase of Saudi Arabia's national renewables program, which aims to generate 9.5 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2023. The initiative aligns with the National Transition Plan 2020 and the objectives outlined in Saudi Vision 2030.

Mohammad Abdullah Abunayyan, chairman of ACWA Power, said the company was "humbled to be entrusted with the first utility-scale renewable energy plant to be developed in the Kingdom, which will be the foundation of the visionary ambitious national program that seeks to maximize the utilization of renewable energy sources."

Abunayyan noted that ACWA Power has previously developed renewable energy projects in the United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Egypt, Morocco and South Africa. He also praised the Renewable Energy Projects Development Office for its professionalism and transparency throughout the procurement process.

The award marks ACWA Power's entry into Saudi Arabia's renewable energy sector as the kingdom moves to diversify its energy portfolio away from oil-dependent power generation.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/ "ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant
content:
Wärtsilä signed a three-year asset management agreement with Saudi Arabia-based Northern Region Cement Company (NRCC) to operate and maintain a power plant in Turaif, extending cooperation that began in 2008.

Under the agreement, Wärtsilä will handle all operational and maintenance aspects of NRCC's 62 MW power plant, including day-to-day operations and preventive and predictive maintenance. The company will remotely monitor the plant's condition through Wärtsilä's Customer Centre in Dubai, providing technical advisory and data analysis services to optimize performance and reliability.

"Wärtsilä has been responsible for the operation and maintenance of our power plant already since 2008. We have been very satisfied with the quality of Wärtsilä's service and support as well as the way they ensure safe and reliable operations," Dr. Obaid Alsobiei, CEO of Northern Region Cement Company, said in a statement.

The continuous remote monitoring enables predictive decision-making to ensure the plant's reliability and efficiency while optimizing fuel consumption. Wärtsilä has previously conducted electrical and automation services to improve performance and extend the facility's lifecycle.

Haidar al Hertani, Managing Director of Wärtsilä Saudi Arabia, said the agreement would give NRCC peace of mind to focus on its core business while the company ensures reliable operations and reduced maintenance costs.

The contract was signed in October 2017.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/ "Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant"

title: Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia
content:
Wärtsilä will supply a 38 MW power plant to a customer in Saudi Arabia, the technology group said on Tuesday. The turn-key engineering, procurement and construction order includes four Wärtsilä 32TS engines and was booked in the first quarter of 2017.

The Wärtsilä 32TS is a two-stage turbocharged engine optimized for extreme ambient conditions. It delivers high efficiency with reduced fuel and water consumption in high altitudes and hot temperatures while meeting World Bank environmental, health and safety guidelines for nitrogen oxide emissions. The power plant will be the first Wärtsilä 32TS installation to operate primarily on crude oil, with heavy and light fuel oils as backup fuels.

Saudi Arabia launched an energy sector transformation programme aligned with its Vision 2030 agenda. The country is pursuing broad initiatives to improve energy efficiency, increase the role of gas and renewables in its energy mix, and effectively utilize existing natural resources.

Qasim Latif, business development manager at Wärtsilä Energy Solutions, said the engine's high efficiency, operational flexibility, multi-fuel capability and minimal water consumption made it well-suited for the Saudi Arabian market. "The ability to act as a state-of-the-art enabler of renewable energy integration" also enhanced its appeal, he added.

Wärtsilä has operated in Saudi Arabia for more than 40 years. Upon completion of this project, the company will have more than 1,900 MW of installed capacity in the country. Globally, Wärtsilä's installed base exceeds 63 GW across 176 countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/

title: MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia
content:
MAN Diesel & Turbo secured an engineering, procurement and construction contract to build a 40 MW power plant for Arabian Cement Company at a cement factory in Rabigh, Saudi Arabia, about 120 km north of Jeddah.

The plant will use two MAN 18V48/60 TS engines fueled by heavy fuel oil and diesel oil. The two-stage turbocharging system in the 48/60 TS engines delivers increased power density and turbocharging efficiency compared to single-turbocharger versions.

Construction of the first phase is scheduled for 2016 and 2017. The facility is designed for expansion, with two additional construction phases planned to add four more engines and increase total capacity to 120 MW.

"MAN Diesel & Turbo has been a reliable partner of ours for many years," said Gasem Al Maimani, President and CEO of Arabian Cement Company. "In addition to a high level of professionalism and the quality of the engines, we appreciate the company's flexibility and capacity for innovation."

As consortium leader, MAN Diesel & Turbo is responsible for constructing the entire plant. The company has completed multiple power plant projects in Saudi Arabia.

In 2016, MAN also planned to hand over a separate 55 MW power plant to United Cement Industrial Company, which would be operated through MAN's service brand PrimeServ.

Ashraf Al Natsheh, Senior Sales Director Power Plants for Saudi Arabia at MAN Diesel & Turbo, said the order "marks another milestone in the development of our EPC business in Saudi Arabia and speaks to the trustful relationship we were able to build with the local cement and building industry."

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/

content:

The event is positioned at the intersection of energy policy and one of the fastest-growing data center regions in the country. POWER magazine, which has covered the power generation industry for more than a century, is the official event organizer. The conference will feature sessions and cross-sector discussions designed to move from identifying constraints to executing solutions within the industry.

Attendees can register online. The event also offers opportunities for companies to exhibit or sponsor. Hotel accommodations are available through a dedicated booking link for the Omni Shoreham.

The 2025 iteration of the conference has concluded, with organizers releasing a recap video of discussions from that year's event.

title: Aramco, Rondo Energy Studying GW-Scale Thermal Storage, Hydrogen, Carbon Capture Deployment
content:
Saudi Aramco and Rondo Energy have entered a memorandum of understanding to conduct engineering studies for an industrial-scale thermal energy storage project that could eventually reach 1 GWh in capacity.

The companies signed the MoU on May 16 to deploy Rondo's heat batteries at Aramco facilities globally, beginning with industrial-scale deployment designed to reduce operating costs and support Aramco's carbon emission reduction goals. The project could subsequently scale to 1 GWh, with assessments underway for additional deployment across Aramco's worldwide operations.

Aramco and Rondo will also explore joint development of novel applications for Rondo's heat battery technology, including hydrogen production and carbon capture capabilities. The Rondo Heat Battery stores energy from wind and solar power and supplies continuous energy at high temperatures needed for industrial processes.

Ali Al-Meshari, Aramco senior vice president of Technology, Oversight and Coordination, said the company has committed to achieving net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 operational greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 across wholly owned and operated assets. "Innovative technologies deployed at scale are needed to reduce the costs of emissions reduction, and we are investing heavily in developing these through our R&D, venture capital and technology deployment programs," Al-Meshari said.

Aramco Ventures previously concluded an equity investment in Rondo Energy in July of last year. The California-headquartered company provides lower-carbon industrial heat and power solutions.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/aramco-rondo-energy-studying-gw-scale-thermal-storage-hydrogen-carbon-capture-deployment/

title: Saudi Group Set to Build 10-GW Wind Farm in Egypt
content:
A Saudi Arabian energy company signed an agreement with Egyptian authorities on July 17 to develop a 10-GW wind farm in Egypt's Sohag region, marking a major expansion of the country's renewable energy capacity.

ACWA Power, a Saudi Arabia-based private energy company, signed a memorandum of understanding with Egypt's New and Renewable Energy Authority to allocate approximately 3,000 square kilometers of land west of Sohag, located on the west bank of the Nile River, for the project. The agreement represents the next phase following an initial accord signed in November 2022 among ACWA Power, the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Company, and the NREA.

Egypt's Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy, H.E. Dr. Mohamed Shaker Al-Marqabi, said the project aligns with Egypt's target to increase renewable energy's share in the power mix to 42% by 2035. "Egypt has adopted an ambitious program to advance the electricity sector in various fields, which includes maximizing the utilization of new and renewable energy resources," Al-Marqabi said.

The minister also characterized the project as strengthening bilateral relations between Egypt and Saudi Arabia, noting the country's comparative advantages in land availability for renewable energy production. Officials said the wind power plant is expected to generate annual savings of approximately $6.5 billion for Egypt.

The project underscores Egypt's efforts to reduce dependence on natural gas for electricity generation while advancing its Vision 2030 development strategy and National Climate Strategy 2050.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-group-set-to-build-10-gw-wind-farm-in-egypt/

title: China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan
content:
China's Envision Energy will supply 1 GW of generation capacity for wind projects that Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power is developing in Uzbekistan, the Shanghai-based turbine manufacturer announced on June 20.

Envision, China's second-largest wind turbine manufacturer, will provide its EN171-6.5 turbines to ACWA's Bash and Dzhankeldy onshore wind farms in Uzbekistan. The announcement follows several large turbine deals by Envision in recent months across Egypt, India, and Saudi Arabia.

The deal comes after Envision was selected to supply approximately 1.7 GW of turbines to the $8.4 billion Neom hydrogen facility in Saudi Arabia. The Neom project will integrate 4 GW of solar, wind, and battery energy storage to produce as much as 600 tonnes daily of green ammonia using more than 2 GW of hydrogen electrolysis capacity.

According to BloombergNEF, Envision was the world's fourth-largest turbine supplier in the previous year, with 8.3 GW of installed generation capacity.

Uzbekistan is expanding its renewable energy capacity as part of a national target to install at least 5 GW of onshore wind by 2030. ACWA Power signed a $10 billion investment cooperation agreement with Uzbekistan's Ministry of Energy in 2023 covering renewable energy, green hydrogen, and gas-to-power projects over five years.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/ "China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan"

title: ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project
content:
ACWA Power inaugurated the 300 MW Sakaka photovoltaic project in Al Jouf, Saudi Arabia on April 9, 2021, marking the kingdom's first utility-scale renewable energy installation.

The project, developed by ACWA Power with an investment value of 1.2 billion Saudi riyals, operated under a consortium structure and was awarded at a tariff of 8.781 halalas/kWh in 2018. The company stated that 97% of the operating team are Saudi nationals, with 90% of the workforce sourced from the Al Jouf region.

The ceremony was attended by Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz Bin Salman Bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, and Al Jouf Governor Prince Faisal bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz.

Prince Abdulaziz stated that the Public Investment Fund holds 50% ownership of ACWA Power and praised the company's role in developing renewable energy across thirteen countries. He emphasized the private sector's importance in advancing the kingdom's renewable energy initiatives.

The Sakaka project represents a key component of Saudi Arabia's National Renewable Energy Program, which aims to lower solar electricity costs across the kingdom. The tariff achieved in the project's award set a world record at the time and enabled subsequent renewable projects to reduce costs further.

ACWA Power is a Saudi-based developer, investor and operator of power generation and water desalination plants. The company has expanded its operations internationally across multiple regions beyond its domestic portfolio.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/ "ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project"

title: Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia
content:
Doosan Škoda Power successfully commissioned two 200 MW steam turbines at the Fadhili combined cycle power plant near Jubail in Saudi Arabia, with the units operating since March 2020.

The two DST-S10 condensation turbines, supplied by the Czech manufacturer alongside control systems, pipelines and auxiliary equipment, will generate 418 MW from the facility's total 1,509 MW capacity. The Fadhili plant operates under joint ownership by Saudi Aramco, Saudi Electricity Company and French utility Engie.

Of the plant's output, 1,100 MW will supply electricity to local industry and residents. The remaining 409 MW supports the power plant's internal operations and gas extraction activities.

Doosan Škoda Power designed the installation to comply with local seismic codes and operate at 60 Hz frequency, differing from European standards. The project was executed in cooperation with the engineering, procurement and construction supplier Doosan Heavy Industries & Construction Co.

"The execution of the project went smoothly, mainly due to synergies and close cooperation within the group with the EPC supplier," said Jindřich Závodný, project manager. "The installation and commissioning were supervised by our experienced technical advisers, who, together with their Korean colleagues, handed over the units for commercial operation."

The units entered a warranty period with ongoing technical support from Doosan Škoda Power to ensure stable operations. The company identified the Fadhili project as its first installation in Saudi Arabia, though it maintains a broader regional presence in the Middle East.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/

title: Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy
content:
Wärtsilä will supply engineering, equipment and technical advisory for a 44 MW power plant at the Mansourah & Massarah Gold Project mine in Saudi Arabia, marking the country's first newbuild power project using a hybrid concept combining engine technology with solar energy.

The order, placed in March by Larsen & Toubro Ltd, the India-based main contractors, calls for six Wärtsilä 32 engines with five units operating and one on constant standby. The engine technology's ability to reach full power output within minutes of startup enables the plant to efficiently integrate fluctuating renewable solar energy while maintaining grid stability.

Amit Swarnkar, MENA business unit head for Larsen & Toubro's Metallurgical and Material Handling Division, said the company selected Wärtsilä technology for its capability to integrate photovoltaic solar into the system. "Reliability is key for any gold mine for securing uninterrupted operations and targeted revenues in all occasions," Swarnkar said in a statement.

Alexandre Eykerman, Energy Business Director Middle East at Wärtsilä, said maximising renewable energy use is central to the company's strategy. "We enhance this energy transition by optimising energy systems for our customers, and have extensive experience in providing power for mining operations, so this project fits our capabilities perfectly," Eykerman said.

Equipment will be delivered on a fast-track schedule within 10 months of contract signing. The plant is scheduled for commissioning by May 2021, with commercial operations beginning a year later.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/

title: ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market
content:
ACWA Power signed an implementation agreement with Azerbaijan's Ministry of Energy to develop, build and operate a 240 MW wind power project in the country, marking the Saudi Arabian company's entry into the Azerbaijani market.

The project will operate on a Build Own Operate basis with a 20-year power purchase agreement. The signing ceremony took place in Baku on January 9, 2020, attended by Azerbaijani Prime Minister Ali Asadov, Energy Minister Parviz Shahbazov, and ACWA Power executives including Chairman Mohammad Abunayyan and Chief Investment Officer Rajit Nanda.

The wind farm represents one of the first two independent power projects in Azerbaijan. It supports the government's strategy to diversify its energy mix and reduce dependence on oil and gas revenues as the country pursues major economic reforms.

"We are pleased to sign this implementation agreement with the Government of Azerbaijan, which expands our geographic footprint of operations into this culturally rich and economically vibrant country straddling Europe and Asia," Paddy Padmanathan, Chief Executive Officer of ACWA Power, said at the ceremony.

Padmanathan added that the investment reinforces ACWA Power's role in deploying Saudi foreign investment to promote decarbonisation of the electricity generation sector and support Azerbaijan's sustainability plans.

ACWA Power said the project would leverage the country's renewable energy resources and apply the company's expertise in reducing renewable energy generation costs, providing affordable energy access to Azerbaijani communities.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/

title: Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East
content:
Solar power projects across the Middle East and North Africa region are expected to add at least $15 billion in generating capacity over the next five years, the Middle East Solar Industry Association said in late September.

The region's solar market is valued at more than $20 billion, with photovoltaic technology now the most competitive form of power generation in the Gulf region, according to the International Renewable Energy Agency. Gulf Cooperation Council countries, including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, plan to install almost 7 GW of new renewable power generation capacity by the early 2020s.

Saudi Arabia has advanced its solar procurement plan, opening bidding for seven projects totaling more than 1.5 GW of capacity. Oman has begun the procurement process for at least 1 GW of residential solar generation. Algeria announced tenders for 2 GW of solar generation.

The UAE leads the region with the largest operational and in-development solar projects, representing 58% of the top five MENA markets, followed by Egypt at 23%, Jordan at 10%, and Morocco at 6%, with Iran accounting for 3%, said Mina Mesbahi, a research analyst at Solarplaza International B.V. in Rotterdam, Netherlands.

The region's year-round sunshine provides favorable conditions for solar development. Approximately 3 GW of operational solar capacity is currently in place across MENA, Mesbahi said.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/ "Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East"

title: NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY
content:
Nationwide Boiler Inc. has secured its largest rental project in its 51-year history, supplying three 200,000 lb/hr watertube boilers for Saudi Aramco's Jizan Refinery in Saudi Arabia.

The Fremont, California-based company will provide the three Babcock & Wilcox package boilers, each rated at 750 psi design and 750F superheated steam on a skid-mounted configuration. Nationwide Boiler will also supply a plant master workstation and one deaerator tank with pumps for the rental duration.

The equipment will support the commissioning of Saudi Aramco's 106-square-kilometer refinery and terminal in Jizan, which is expected to process more than 400,000 barrels-per-day of crude oil. The facility will be entirely self-sufficient, providing power to locally owned manufacturing and service companies.

"Having three high capacity watertube boilers complete with immediate availability was key in securing the order, but a significant amount of preparation, multiple discussions to formulate an offering that fit their needs best, and a committed team with all hands-on deck is really what sealed the deal," said William Testa, Nationwide Boiler's Director of Sales.

The boilers will be utilized for a minimum of one year during the refinery's commissioning phase. The equipment will ship from Houston on a roll-on/roll-off vessel with an expected transit time of six weeks to the Port of Jizan. Nationwide Boiler was assisted in the project by Middle East representative Aujan Industrial Solutions and EPC customer Redland Industrial Services (Arabia) Ltd.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/

title: ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia
content:
ACWA Power has won Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy project, a 300 MW solar facility in Al Jouf that will set a new world record tariff for photovoltaic power.

The Skaka PV solar project was awarded to ACWA Power under a 25-year power purchase contract at 8.782 halala per kilowatt-hour, equivalent to U.S. 2.34 cents/kWh. The plant, located at a site spanning more than six square kilometers, carries an investment value of SR 993,750 million (US $256 million).

The project marks the first procurement under Saudi Arabia's national renewables program, which aims to produce 9.5 GW of renewable energy by 2023 as a first phase. The initiative aligns with the Kingdom's Vision 2030 and National Transition Plan 2020.

Mohammad Abdullah Abunayyan, chairman of ACWA Power, said the company is "proud to have set yet another world record tariff for PV power, now at home, and are humbled to be entrusted with the first utility-scale renewable energy plant to be developed in the Kingdom."

Abunayyan said ACWA Power brings experience from renewable energy projects across the United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Egypt, Morocco and South Africa. He added that the company will "bring to the Kingdom our considerable expertise and capabilities to ensure the delivery of this first renewable energy project at the highest global standards of quality and efficiency."

ACWA Power operates power generation and desalination plants across 11 countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/ "ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant
content:
Wärtsilä has extended its asset management agreement with Saudi Arabia's Northern Region Cement Company by three years to operate and maintain NRCC's 62 MW power plant in Turaif. The agreement, signed in October 2017, marks a continuation of the companies' partnership that began in 2008.

Under the expanded contract, Wärtsilä will handle all aspects of plant operations, including day-to-day management, preventive maintenance and predictive maintenance services. The company will remotely monitor the power plant's condition from its Customer Centre in Dubai, using real-time data to optimize performance and provide technical advisory services.

"Wärtsilä has been responsible for the operation and maintenance of our power plant already since 2008. We have been very satisfied with the quality of Wärtsilä's service and support as well as the way they ensure safe and reliable operations," Dr. Obaid Alsobiei, CEO of Northern Region Cement Company, said in a statement.

Wärtsilä has previously undertaken electrical and automation services upgrades to enhance performance and extend the plant's operational lifespan. The company said the arrangement has enabled optimized fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC.

"By having full responsibility for the operation and maintenance of NRCC's power plant, we have been able to ensure reliable operations, optimised fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC," Haidar al Hertani, Managing Director of Wärtsilä Saudi Arabia, said.

The contract covers continuous remote monitoring and knowledge-based decision-making to ensure reliability and efficiency of the cement company's power generation facility.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/ "Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant"

title: Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia
content:
Wärtsilä will supply a 38 MW power plant to a Saudi Arabian customer as a turn-key engineering, procurement and construction order booked in the first quarter of 2017. The project includes four Wärtsilä 32TS engines, a two-stage turbocharged variant optimized for extreme ambient conditions.

The 32TS engine delivers high efficiency with reduced fuel and water consumption in high altitudes and hot temperatures while meeting World Bank environmental, health and safety guidelines for nitrogen oxide emissions. The power plant will be the first Wärtsilä 32TS installation to operate primarily on crude oil, with heavy and light fuel oils as backup fuels.

The project aligns with Saudi Arabia's broader energy sector transformation programme, including initiatives to improve energy efficiency and increase the role of gas and renewables in the energy mix. It supports the country's Vision 2030 programme.

Qasim Latif, Business Development Manager at Wärtsilä Energy Solutions, said the solution's high efficiency, operational flexibility, multi-fuel capability and minimal water consumption make it well-suited for the Saudi Arabian market. He added that the technology can enable renewable energy integration.

Wärtsilä has operated in Saudi Arabia for more than 40 years. Upon completion of this project, the company will have more than 1,900 MW of installed capacity in the country. Globally, Wärtsilä's installed base spans over 63 GW across 176 countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/

title: MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia
content:
MAN Diesel & Turbo has secured an engineering, procurement and construction contract to build a 40 MW power plant for Arabian Cement Company in Rabigh, Saudi Arabia, about 120 kilometers north of Jeddah on the Red Sea coast.

The plant will use two MAN 18V48/60 TS engines and operate on heavy fuel oil and diesel oil to supply power to a cement factory. The engines feature two-stage turbocharging, which the company said increases efficiency, power density and turbocharging performance compared to single-turbocharger versions.

Construction of the first phase is scheduled for 2016-2017. Arabian Cement Company plans to expand the facility in subsequent phases, adding four more engines to increase total capacity to 120 MW.

Gasem Al Maimani, president and CEO of Arabian Cement Company, said, "MAN Diesel & Turbo has been a reliable partner of ours for many years. In addition to a high level of professionalism and the quality of the engines, we appreciate the company's flexibility and capacity for innovation."

As EPC consortium leader, MAN Diesel & Turbo will manage the entire plant construction. The company said it has extensive experience with similar projects in the region.

Ashraf Al Natsheh, Senior Sales Director Power Plants for Saudi Arabia at MAN Diesel & Turbo, said the order represents "another milestone in the development of our EPC business in Saudi Arabia" and reflects strong relationships with local cement and building industry clients.

In 2016, MAN will also hand over a 55 MW power plant to United Cement Industrial Company, which will be operated by MAN's service brand PrimeServ.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/

title: Saudi Arabia Innovates to Support Renewable Energy Goals
content:
Saudi Arabia is pursuing advanced thermal management solutions for solar installations as part of broader efforts to meet renewable energy targets outlined in its Vision 2030 strategy.

The country aims to generate 50% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030, including 58.7 GW from solar energy and 40 GW from wind energy, totaling 130 GW of renewable capacity. High-efficiency triple-junction photovoltaic cells, which convert solar energy more effectively than conventional systems, face significant performance degradation in high-temperature environments common to the region's arid climate.

Thermal management has become critical for maintaining the efficiency and durability of advanced PV systems. Active cooling technologies mitigate heat-related issues including thermal degradation and uneven heat distribution that reduce output and reliability. By integrating cooling systems into solar installations, operators can maintain performance closer to optimal efficiency levels in the region's intense heat conditions.

Saudi Arabia's renewable energy expansion supports Vision 2030, a strategic initiative aimed at reducing oil dependency and diversifying the economy. The initiative emphasizes sustainability and clean energy development alongside fostering innovation in knowledge-based sectors. Investing in advanced cooling technologies for solar systems aligns with these goals while positioning the kingdom as a leader in renewable energy innovation.

The scale of Saudi Arabia's solar ambitions, combined with the technical challenges posed by its climate, underscore the importance of developing efficient cooling solutions for large-scale PV deployment. Such technological advances will be essential for achieving stated renewable capacity targets while maximizing the return on energy infrastructure investments.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/saudi-arabia-innovates-to-support-renewable-energy-goals/

title: Carbon Capture Projects Come Online in Iceland, Saudi Arabia
content:
Carbon capture facilities have come online in Iceland and Saudi Arabia, marking a new phase in direct removal of greenhouse gases from industrial emissions and the atmosphere.

Officials in Iceland celebrated the opening of Steingerdur, a carbon capture installation at the ON Power geothermal plant that is expected to deliver about 10% of Iceland's 2030 Climate Action Plan targets for the energy and industrial sectors. The facility uses direct water capture technology to remove carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide emissions from the geothermal steam cycle, then stores the minerals underground using technology from Carbfix, where they turn into rock in about two years.

Iceland is also home to Orca, a direct air capture and storage facility operated by Switzerland-based Climeworks. The plant came online in September 2021.

In Saudi Arabia, Climeworks, the King Abdullah Petroleum Studies and Research Center (KAPSARC), and the Saudi Arabia Energy Ministry inaugurated a direct air capture testing unit at KAPSARC's facilities in Riyadh. The mobile unit captures carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere and is validating Climeworks' technology under hot and arid conditions.

Climeworks began operating a U.S. headquarters in Austin, Texas, last year. ON Power operates geothermal and hydropower plants in Iceland. Both ON Power and Carbfix are subsidiaries of Reykjavík Energy.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/carbon-capture-projects-come-online-in-iceland-saudi-arabia/

content:

Aramco aims to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and Scope 2 operational greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 across its wholly owned and operated assets. Ali Al-Meshari, Aramco senior vice president of Technology, Oversight and Coordination, said the company is investing heavily in emissions reduction technologies through research and development, venture capital and technology deployment programs.

Aramco Ventures previously concluded an equity investment in Rondo Energy in July 2023. The California-based company develops lower-carbon industrial heat and power solutions for energy-intensive industries.

content:

Egypt's Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy H.E. Dr. Mohamed Shaker Al-Marqabi said in a statement that the project aligns with Egypt's Vision 2030 and National Climate Strategy 2050. He added that Egypt aims to increase renewable energy's share in its energy mix to 42% by 2035.
