title: China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan
content:
China's Envision Energy will supply 1 GW of generation capacity for wind projects in Uzbekistan designed by Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power, the Shanghai-based turbine manufacturer announced on June 20.

Envision, China's second-largest wind turbine manufacturer, will provide its EN171-6.5 turbines to ACWA's Bash and Dzhankeldy onshore wind farms in Uzbekistan. The announcement follows several large wind turbine deals by Envision in recent months across Egypt, India and Saudi Arabia.

The company in mid-June was selected to supply approximately 1.7 GW of turbines to the $8.4 billion Neom hydrogen facility in Saudi Arabia. That project will integrate 4 GW of solar, wind and battery energy storage to produce up to 600 tonnes daily of green ammonia using more than 2 GW of hydrogen electrolysis capacity.

BloombergNEF ranked Envision as the world's fourth-largest turbine supplier last year, with 8.3 GW of installed generation capacity. The company also leads among Chinese manufacturers in energy management software.

Uzbekistan is expanding renewable energy deployment as part of broader energy transition efforts. The country targets at least 5 GW of onshore wind capacity by 2030. ACWA Power signed a $10 billion investment cooperation agreement with Uzbekistan's Ministry of Energy in 2023 covering renewable energy, green hydrogen and gas-to-power projects over five years.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/china-saudi-arabia-team-on-wind-projects-in-uzbekistan/ "China, Saudi Arabia Team on Wind Projects in Uzbekistan"

title: ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project
content:
ACWA Power inaugurated Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy project on April 9, 2021, with the 300 MW Sakaka photovoltaic facility in Al Jouf.

The project, developed at an investment value of 1.2 billion Saudi riyals, was officially opened under the patronage of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz Bin Salman Bin Abdulaziz Al Saud and Al Jouf Region Governor Prince Faisal bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz also attended the ceremony.

ACWA Power, a Saudi developer and operator of power generation and water desalination plants, led the project consortium. The Public Investment Fund holds 50% ownership of ACWA Power, which operates in 13 countries globally.

The Saudi Minister of Energy stated that 97% of the operating team are Saudis, with 90% of the workforce from the Al Jouf region. He emphasized the private sector's role in advancing renewable energy development in the Kingdom.

The Sakaka project was awarded to the ACWA Power-led consortium at a tariff of 8.781 halalas per kilowatt-hour in 2018, which represented a world record at the time. The competitive tariff helped establish benchmarks for subsequent projects under Saudi Arabia's National Renewable Energy Program, supporting the Kingdom's broader energy diversification strategy.

The inauguration marked a significant step in the country's plans to increase renewable energy capacity as part of its Vision 2030 economic transformation program.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-inaugurates-renewable-energy-project/ "ACWA Power Inaugurates Renewable Energy Project"

title: Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia
content:
Doosan Škoda Power has launched two 200 MW steam turbines at the Fadhili power plant near Jubail in Saudi Arabia, with both units operating since March 2020.

The turbines are DST-S10 condensation units with axial output and no reheating. They produce 418 MW of the total 1,509 MW output from the Fadhili steam-gas combined cycle plant, a facility jointly owned by Saudi Aramco, Saudi Electricity Company and French firm Engie. Of the plant's total capacity, 1,100 MW supplies electricity to local industry and residents, while 400 MW supports the power plant's operations and gas extraction.

Doosan Škoda Power supplied the turbines along with associated equipment including pipelines, control systems with field equipment, steam compression and drainage systems, oil systems and high-pressure hydraulic systems. The Czech manufacturer worked with EPC supplier Doosan Heavy Industries & Construction Co. on the project.

Project manager Jindřich Závodný said the execution proceeded smoothly due to cooperation within the group. The installation had to comply with Saudi Arabia's 60 Hz electrical frequency standard and local seismic codes. Experienced technical advisers from both companies supervised installation and commissioning, with the units now in commercial operation, he said.

The plant is currently under warranty with Doosan Škoda Power providing complete support to ensure stable operation. This marks the company's first installation in Saudi Arabia, though its turbines already operate at other Middle East projects including Zarqa.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/doosan-skoda-power-successfully-launched-two-200-mw-turbines-at-the-fadhili-power-plant-in-saudia-arabia/ "Doosan Škoda Power successfully launched two 200 MW turbines at the Fadhili power plant in Saudia Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy
content:
Wärtsilä will supply engineering, equipment and technical advisory for a 44 MW hybrid power plant at the Mansourah & Massarah Gold Project mine in Saudi Arabia, marking the country's first newbuild power project combining engine technology with solar energy.

Larsen & Toubro Ltd, the India-based main contractors, placed the order with Wärtsilä in March. The power plant will comprise six Wärtsilä 32 engines, with five in operation and one on constant standby.

The plant's design leverages Wärtsilä engine technology's ability to reach full power output within minutes of startup, enabling efficient integration of fluctuating renewable solar energy supply. Renewable sources like solar and wind require power plants to respond rapidly to variable generation to maintain effective load balancing.

Amit Swarnkar, MENA Business Unit Head of Metallurgical and Material Handling Division at Larsen & Toubro, said the company selected Wärtsilä technology specifically for its capability to integrate photovoltaic solar into the system. He cited reliability as essential for uninterrupted mining operations and securing revenue in all circumstances.

Alexandre Eykerman, Energy Business Director Middle East at Wärtsilä, said maximising renewable energy use is central to the company's approach to enabling sustainable energy supplies. Wärtsilä has extensive experience providing power for mining operations, he added.

Wärtsilä will deliver equipment on a fast-track basis within 10 months of contract signing. The plant is scheduled for commissioning by May 2021, with commercial operations beginning a year later.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-engine-technology-will-enable-saudi-arabia-mining-site-power-plant-to-efficiently-utilize-solar-energy/ "Wärtsilä engine technology will enable Saudi Arabia mining site power plant to efficiently utilize solar energy"

title: ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market
content:
ACWA Power signed an implementation agreement with Azerbaijan's Ministry of Energy to develop, build and operate a 240 MW wind power project, marking the company's entry into the country's energy market.

The power purchase agreement spans 20 years on a Build Own Operate basis, with the signing ceremony attended by Azerbaijan's Prime Minister Ali Asadov and Energy Minister Parviz Shahbazov on January 9, 2020, in Baku.

The project is one of Azerbaijan's first two independent power projects and aligns with government efforts to diversify the country's energy mix away from oil and gas dependence. ACWA Power, a Saudi Arabian company, will leverage Azerbaijan's renewable energy resources to strengthen the nation's energy security.

"We are pleased to sign this implementation agreement with the Government of Azerbaijan, which expands our geographic footprint of operations into this culturally rich and economically vibrant country," said Paddy Padmanathan, Chief Executive Officer of ACWA Power.

Padmanathan added that the investment in the renewable energy project reinforces ACWA Power's commitment to supporting decarbonization efforts across multiple countries pursuing sustainability goals.

The 240 MW wind facility is expected to reduce renewable energy generation costs and provide affordable electricity access to Azerbaijani communities while supporting broader economic development in the country.

ACWA Power brings operational expertise in renewable energy projects and cost reduction strategies to the initiative, according to the company's statement.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-expands-its-geographic-footprint-by-entering-the-azerbaijan-market/ "ACWA Power expands its geographic footprint by entering the Azerbaijan market"

title: Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East
content:
Solar power projects are expanding rapidly across the Middle East and North Africa, with the Middle East Solar Industry Association forecasting at least $15 billion of new solar capacity will enter operation in the region over the next five years.

MESIA estimated the region's solar market is worth more than $20 billion. Solar photovoltaic technology has become the most competitive form of power generation in the Gulf region, according to the International Renewable Energy Agency. Gulf Cooperation Council countries, including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, plan to install almost 7 GW of new renewable power generation capacity by the early 2020s.

Saudi Arabia advanced to a second phase of its solar procurement plan this year, opening bidding for seven projects totaling more than 1.5 GW. Oman launched procurement for at least 1 GW of residential solar generation. Algeria announced tenders for 2 GW of solar generation capacity.

The UAE leads the MENA region in operational and planned solar projects, accounting for 58% of the region's pipeline, according to Mina Mesbahi, research analyst at Solarplaza International B.V. Egypt follows with 23%, Jordan with 10%, Morocco with 6%, and Iran with 3%. The region has approximately 3 GW of operational solar capacity.

"Almost all the countries in this region experience an all year-round sunshine, providing breeding grounds for solar development," Mesbahi said.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/solar-projects-show-rapid-growth-in-middle-east/ "Solar Projects Show Rapid Growth in Middle East"

title: NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY
content:
Nationwide Boiler Inc. secured its largest rental project in 51 years by supplying three steam boilers to Saudi Aramco's new Jizan Refinery in Saudi Arabia.

The company will provide three 200,000 lb/hr watertube boilers rated at 750 psi design and 750F superheated steam for a minimum one-year rental during the refinery's commissioning phase. Nationwide Boiler, a distributor of Babcock & Wilcox package boilers, also will supply a plant master workstation and one deaerator tank with pumps.

The equipment will support operations at the 106-square-kilometer refinery and terminal, which is expected to process more than 400,000 barrels-per-day of crude oil for domestic and international customers. The facility will be entirely self-sufficient, providing power to locally owned manufacturing and service companies.

William Testa, Nationwide Boiler's Director of Sales, said the company's immediate availability of complete, in-stock boilers was critical to winning the contract. "Having three high capacity watertube boilers complete with immediate availability was key in securing the order, but a significant amount of preparation, multiple discussions to formulate an offering that fit their needs best, and a committed team with 'all hands-on deck' is really what sealed the deal," Testa stated.

Nationwide Boiler was assisted by Aujan Industrial Solutions, its Middle East representative, and Redland Industrial Services (Arabia) Ltd., the EPC customer. The boilers will ship from the Port of Houston via a Ro-Ro vessel, with an expected transit time of six weeks before arrival at the Port of Jizan.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/nationwide-boiler-inc-secures-largest-rental-project-in-their-fifty-one-year-history/ "NATIONWIDE BOILER INC. SECURES LARGEST RENTAL PROJECT IN THEIR FIFTY-ONE YEAR HISTORY"

title: ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia
content:
ACWA Power has won Saudi Arabia's first utility-scale renewable energy project, a 300 MW solar plant in Al Jouf that will operate under a 25-year power purchase contract at a tariff of 8.782 halala per kilowatt-hour, or 2.34 US cents per kWh.

The Skaka solar project marks the beginning of Saudi Arabia's national renewable energy program, which aims to produce 9.5 GW of renewable capacity by 2023 in its first phase. The plant will cover more than six square kilometers and require an investment of SR 993,750 million, equivalent to US $256 million.

Mohammad Abdullah Abunayyan, chairman of ACWA Power, said the company was "proud to have set yet another world record tariff for PV power, now at home," and described the award as humbling given the plant's role as the foundation for the kingdom's renewable energy expansion under the National Transition Plan 2020 and Saudi Vision 2030.

Abunayyan noted that ACWA Power brings experience from similar projects in the United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Egypt, Morocco and South Africa. The company operates power generation and desalination plants across 11 countries.

The Renewable Energy Projects Development Office managed the procurement process, which the ACWA Power chairman praised for its professionalism, transparency and integrity throughout prequalification and final selection.

The Skaka project represents the opening phase of what the Saudi government envisions as multiple renewable energy procurements under its broader energy transition strategy.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/acwa-power-wins-first-utility-scale-renewable-energy-project-in-saudi-arabia/ "ACWA Power Wins First Utility-Scale Renewable Energy Project in Saudi Arabia"

title: Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant
content:
Wärtsilä signed a three-year asset management agreement in October 2017 with Saudi Arabia-based Northern Region Cement Company (NRCC) to operate and maintain NRCC's 62 MW power plant in Turaif.

The agreement extends a partnership that began in 2008, with Wärtsilä responsible for day-to-day operations, preventive and predictive maintenance, and electrical and automation services. The Finnish technology group's Customer Centre in Dubai continuously monitors the power plant remotely to provide real-time data analysis and technical advisory services.

"Wärtsilä has been responsible for the operation and maintenance of our power plant already since 2008. We have been very satisfied with the quality of Wärtsilä's service and support as well as the way they ensure safe and reliable operations," said Dr. Obaid Alsobiei, CEO of NRCC.

Wärtsilä said the remote monitoring capabilities enable knowledge-based, predictive decisions to optimize the plant's reliability and efficiency. The company has also carried out electrical and automation upgrades to improve performance and extend the power plant's lifecycle.

"By having full responsibility for the operation and maintenance of NRCC's power plant, we have been able to ensure reliable operations, optimised fuel consumption and reduced maintenance costs for NRCC," said Haidar al Hertani, Managing Director of Wärtsilä Saudi Arabia.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-ensures-the-reliability-and-availability-of-saudi-arabian-power-plant/ "Wärtsilä ensures the reliability and availability of Saudi Arabian power plant"

title: Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia
content:
Wärtsilä has secured an order to supply a 38 MW power plant to a Saudi Arabian customer, the technology group said. The turn-key engineering, procurement and construction order includes four Wärtsilä 32TS engines and was booked in the first quarter of 2017.

The Wärtsilä 32TS is a two-stage turbocharged engine optimised for extreme ambient conditions. It delivers high efficiency with reduced fuel and water consumption at high altitudes and hot temperatures while meeting World Bank environmental, health and safety guidelines for nitrogen oxide emissions. The power plant will be the first Wärtsilä 32TS installation to operate primarily on crude oil, with heavy and light fuel oils as backup fuels.

The project aligns with Saudi Arabia's broader energy sector transformation programme, which aims to improve energy efficiency, increase the role of gas and renewables, and better utilise existing natural resources. The initiative supports the Saudi Vision 2030 programme.

Qasim Latif, Business Development Manager at Wärtsilä Energy Solutions, said the company's solutions combine high efficiency with operational flexibility, multi-fuel capability and minimal water consumption. "The ability to act as a state-of-the-art enabler of renewable energy integration make our solutions very well-suited for the Saudi Arabian market," Latif said.

Wärtsilä has operated in Saudi Arabia for more than 40 years. Upon completion of this project, the company will have more than 1,900 MW of installed capacity in the country. Globally, Wärtsilä's installed base exceeds 63 GW across 176 countries.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/wartsila-to-supply-38-mw-power-plant-to-saudi-arabia/ "Wärtsilä to supply 38 MW power plant to Saudi Arabia"

title: MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia
content:
MAN Diesel & Turbo has secured an engineering, procurement and construction contract to build a 40 MW power plant for Arabian Cement Company at its facility in Rabigh, Saudi Arabia, about 120 kilometers north of Jeddah.

The plant will use two MAN 18V48/60 TS engines running on heavy fuel oil and diesel oil. The two-stage turbocharged engines are designed to deliver higher power density and increased efficiency compared to single-turbocharger versions.

Construction of the first phase is scheduled for 2016 and 2017. The facility is planned to expand to 120 MW total capacity through two additional construction phases, which will add four more engines to the site.

Gasem Al Maimani, President and CEO of Arabian Cement Company, said the company valued MAN Diesel & Turbo's professionalism, engine quality and ability to meet complex customer requirements. MAN Diesel & Turbo has worked with the customer for several years.

Ashraf Al Natsheh, Senior Sales Director Power Plants for Saudi Arabia at MAN Diesel & Turbo, said the order demonstrated the company's growing position in the region's cement and building industries. In 2016, MAN is also scheduled to hand over a separate 55 MW power plant to United Cement Industrial Company, to be operated by MAN's service brand PrimeServ.

Alexander Stöckler, Global Head of Sales at the MAN Diesel & Turbo power plant division, said Saudi Arabia represents a core growth market where the company has completed numerous successful projects.

Source: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/
source_url: https://www.powermag.com/press-releases/man-diesel-turbo-builds-power-plant-in-saudi-arabia/ "MAN Diesel & Turbo builds power plant in Saudi Arabia"

title: EDF power solutions North America and Masdar Sign Agreements for Solar and Energy Storage Project
content:
EDF power solutions North America and Masdar have signed 15-year power purchase agreements to supply electricity from the BigBeau Solar+Storage Project in Kern County, California, starting Feb. 1, 2026.

The project comprises a 128 megawatt solar plant and a 40 MW/160 MWh battery energy storage system. BigBeau has been operational since December 2022 and will now deliver power to Southern California Edison under the new agreements.

Dustin Priemer, Director Asset Management Masdar Americas, said the agreement reflects the company's focus on scaling utility-scale clean power in the United States. "We are appreciative of our growing partnership with Southern California Edison and our shared commitment to investing in new generation capacity to meet growing energy demand in California," he said.

Jacqueline de Fresart, Associate Director of Origination and Power Marketing at EDF power solutions North America, added that the company was pleased to support Southern California Edison's clean energy goals while providing reliable energy from the operating project.

BigBeau is one of seven projects that Masdar and EDF power solutions agreed to develop together, with combined capacity of 1.1 gigawatts. EDF power solutions has developed 26 gigawatts of wind, solar and storage projects over more than 35 years in North America. Masdar, active in the United States since 2019, has invested several billion dollars in the country and plans to develop a portfolio of up to 25 GW within the next decade.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project
source_url: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project

title: RWE and Masdar receive planning permission for 3GW Dogger Bank South offshore wind farm projects
content:
RWE and Masdar have received planning permission for two offshore wind farms with a combined capacity of 3 gigawatts off the northeast coast of England. Lord Whitehead, acting on behalf of the UK Energy Secretary, granted the Development Consent Order for the Dogger Bank South East and West projects on May 14, 2026.

The two 1.5GW projects are being developed as a joint venture between RWE, which holds 51% of the partnership, and Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company PJSC, or Masdar, with 49%. The wind farms will be located over 100 kilometres offshore and could generate enough electricity to power approximately three million UK homes annually.

Both projects secured Contracts for Difference through the UK Government's Allocation Round 7 in January 2026. The Development Consent Order application was initially submitted in June 2024, with over 1,000 documents reviewed by the Planning Inspectorate and 10 online examination hearings conducted.

RWE and Masdar said they will now undertake detailed final design work and advance procurement decisions. The companies aim to reach a final investment decision in 2027 before moving toward construction.

The wind farms will create business opportunities for communities in Yorkshire, the Humber region, and wider UK locations. The project website notes that both DBS West and DBS East are co-located in the same offshore area.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects
source_url: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects

title: Allocation Round 7 (AR7)
content:
The UK government awarded RWE contracts for difference at £91.20 per megawatt hour for 6.9 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity across five projects in Allocation Round 7, the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero announced on January 14.

RWE secured 20-year contracts for Norfolk Vanguard East and Norfolk Vanguard West, both Dogger Bank South projects, and Awel y Môr. The strike price is in 2024 prices with inflation indexation.

RWE and investment firm KKR agreed to jointly develop, construct and operate Norfolk Vanguard East and Norfolk Vanguard West. KKR will acquire a 50% equity stake in each project. The partnership is expected to close in summer 2026, subject to customary approvals.

Markus Krebber, CEO of RWE AG, said the company was "delighted to have been successful in securing long-term offtake contracts for five projects in AR7." He added that combining KKR's investment expertise with RWE's offshore wind knowledge positions the partners to realise the major projects.

RWE is developing the Dogger Bank South projects with Masdar. It is collaborating on Awel y Môr with Stadtwerke München and Siemens.

The successful AR7 outcome and KKR partnership represent key milestones in executing RWE's UK offshore wind pipeline, Krebber said.

Source: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/
source_url: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/

title: EWEC and Masdar Forge Strategic Framework to Rapidly Scale Renewable Energy and Drive National Decarbonisation Goals
content:
Emirates Water and Electricity Company (EWEC) and Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company Masdar have signed a strategic Collaboration Framework Agreement to accelerate deployment of more than 30GW of solar PV capacity and over 8GW of battery storage across the United Arab Emirates.

The agreement, signed by EWEC Chief Executive Officer Ahmed Ali Alshamsi and Masdar Chief Executive Officer Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, establishes a roadmap for utility-scale renewable energy projects to diversify the UAE's energy mix and strengthen supply security. The framework streamlines Masdar's participation in EWEC's renewable energy project engagement process from early-stage development through financial close while preserving the transparency and independence of EWEC's competitive procurement processes.

EWEC aims to meet 60% of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035 through the expanded solar and battery storage capacity. The agreement builds on previous partnerships between the companies on projects including Al Dhafra Solar PV, Al Ajban Solar PV, and Khazna Solar PV.

"This Collaboration Framework Agreement with Masdar is a pivotal strategic tool that empowers us to accelerate this transformation and meet 60 per cent of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035," Ahmed Ali Alshamsi said. "By streamlining the development of utility-scale solar PV capacity, forecast to exceed 30GW by 2035, and integrating more than 8GW of battery energy storage, we are reshaping the sector while ensuring the long-term security of supply."

The framework is designed to maximise in-country value and foster Emirati talent and expertise while reducing costs and optimising operations.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/wec-and-masdar-forge-strategic-framework-to-scale-renewable-energy
source_url: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/wec-and-masdar-forge-strategic-framework-to-scale-renewable-energy

title: Masdar and EPCG to form joint venture to enhance energy security in Montenegro, the Western Balkans and Southern Europe
content:
Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company PJSC, known as Masdar, and Elektroprivreda Crne Gore, Montenegro's national power utility, agreed to form a 50/50 joint venture to develop large-scale renewable energy projects in Montenegro.

The partnership, announced at the Adria Future Summit, will be headquartered in Nikšić. Masdar and EPCG will deploy and operate clean energy projects across multiple technologies including solar photovoltaic, wind, hydropower, pumped hydro energy storage, stand-alone battery energy storage systems and hybrid solutions.

The joint venture is designed to support Montenegro's domestic energy needs while enabling the export of renewable electricity to the Western Balkans and Southern Europe, leveraging an existing sub-sea interconnection with Italy.

The agreement was signed by Milutin Đukanović, President of the Board of Directors of EPCG, and Zdravko Dragaš, CEO of EPCG, alongside Ahmed Al Awadi, Advisor of the Office of the COO at Masdar. Montenegro's Minister of Energy and Mining Admir Šahmanović attended the signing ceremony.

The partnership combines EPCG's position as Montenegro's leading energy producer with Masdar's global expertise in renewable energy project development. "This partnership represents a turning point for the energy future of Montenegro," Šahmanović said in a statement. "By joining forces, Masdar and EPCG bring together global knowledge, strong national capacity and a shared ambition to speed up the transition towards clean energy."

The collaboration builds on Masdar's existing presence in Montenegro and complements the company's wider regional expansion across Southern Europe.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-and-epcg-to-form-joint-venture
source_url: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-and-epcg-to-form-joint-venture

title: ScottishPower and Masdar set UK record for biggest blade install at East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm
content:
ScottishPower and Masdar have installed the first turbine at their East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm, marking a UK record with 115-metre blades, the largest manufactured and used in the country.

The 1.4 GW project, located off the Suffolk coast, will feature 95 turbines standing approximately 262 metres tall with a rotor diameter of 236 metres. All 285 blades for the 14MW Siemens Gamesa turbines are being manufactured at the company's facility in Hull.

A single turbine revolution will generate enough electricity to power a UK home for more than four days, charge around 1,700 mobile phones, or brew nearly a thousand cups of tea.

When complete, the £4 billion project will produce enough clean power for more than 1.3 million homes and will be one of the world's largest offshore windfarms.

Charlie Jordan, ScottishPower Renewables CEO, said the installation represented "a defining moment and a UK industry first" and noted the project would involve "billions of pounds invested in UK and global supply chains" and support "thousands of jobs during construction" plus "more than a hundred long-term roles created in the East of England."

Jordan added that East Anglia THREE would "play a crucial role in the UK's clean energy future" and reflected successful collaboration between ScottishPower, parent company Iberdrola and strategic investment partner Masdar.

The project is expected to enhance UK energy security by delivering additional clean power to the grid.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three
source_url: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three

title: https://wiser.ae/
content:
WiSER, a global platform established in 2015 by Masdar and the Zayed Sustainability Prize, operates three core programs designed to develop women leaders in the energy transition and sustainability sectors.

The WiSER Pioneers Program, a year-long initiative targeting women aged 25-35, has graduated more than 150 professional young women representing over 30 nationalities. Participants receive customized educational workshops, mentoring and networking opportunities with industry experts to drive net-zero transformation efforts.

The platform has delivered 565 hours of mentoring to young women and conducted 540 hours of workshops with Pioneers participants. WiSER has also convened more than 4,000 influential figures to advance gender parity and sustainability goals.

The WiSER Mentorship Program pairs Pioneers graduates with senior industry mentors to support career development and leadership growth. The WiSER Cares program combines cultural exchange with hands-on learning across five pillars: Leadership, Innovation, Energy Nexus, Community Engagement and Global Network.

WiSER operates from the premise that women disproportionately affected by environmental and development challenges can serve as powerful agents of change. The platform is guided by the UAE's vision and the legacy of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan.

The organization will hold its Annual Forum focused on igniting a global sustainable economy on January 1, 2025.

Source: https://wiser.ae/

title: TotalEnergies and Masdar to form $2.2 billion Joint Venture to Accelerate Renewable Energy Growth in Asia
content:
TotalEnergies and Masdar have signed a binding agreement to establish a $2.2 billion 50/50 joint venture that will merge their onshore renewable activities across nine Asian countries, the companies said on April 2.

The joint venture will operate in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Uzbekistan. Each partner will contribute assets of comparable value to the venture, which will serve as the sole vehicle for developing, building, owning and operating onshore solar, wind and battery storage projects in these markets.

The JV will have 3 GW of operational capacity once the transaction closes, with an additional 6 GW of assets in advanced development expected to become operational by 2030.

"Asia will be the main driver of global electricity demand growth this decade, and this collaboration with TotalEnergies will accelerate our progress across the continent," said His Excellency Dr. Sultan Al Jaber, UAE Minister of Industry and Advanced Technology and Chairman of Masdar.

The partnership reflects accelerating electricity demand across Asia. TotalEnergies has expanded its renewable portfolio in Central Asia and the Caucuses, while Masdar has developed positions in growth markets across the Asia-Pacific region. The companies said the joint venture would allow them to secure significant positions in these markets by combining their operational and financial strength.

The transaction is expected to close subject to customary regulatory approvals and closing conditions.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar
source_url: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar

title: Exus Renewables to acquire stake in Masdar’s Portuguese wind portfolio
content:
Exus Renewables has agreed to acquire a 60% stake in Masdar's portfolio of nine wind farms in northern and central Portugal, with Masdar retaining 40% ownership.

The portfolio, located in Guarda and Castelo Branco, is undergoing repowering that will increase its capacity from 144MW to 164MW. Completion is expected by 2027. Once operational, the repowered sites will generate electricity for more than 200,000 households and avoid approximately 41.7 kilotons of CO₂ emissions annually.

Luis Adão da Fonseca, CEO at Exus Renewables, said the deal "significantly strengthens Exus' operational presence across Europe and underscores our innovative approach to maximising the performance and value of renewable assets."

The partners plan to add 110MW of solar capacity through hybridization at the sites, to be codeveloped by both companies. Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, Chief Executive Officer of Masdar, said the project demonstrates the company's approach to "enhancing the performance and long-term value of these sites" and reflects its focus on "disciplined portfolio management and partnership."

Repowering allows operators to install more efficient turbines at existing sites without requiring new environmental permits, reducing development timelines. Portugal has designated wind energy expansion as a priority for meeting its clean energy targets.

The transaction supports both companies' European expansion strategies while contributing to Portugal's energy transition goals.

title: Power to Change film series
content:
Masdar is driving one of Central Asia's most ambitious renewable energy programs in Uzbekistan, according to the company's latest Power to Change documentary released on November 13.

Since entering Uzbekistan in 2018, Masdar has committed over US$2 billion across major solar and wind developments. These include the 500MW Zarafshan Wind Farm, the 457MW Sherabad solar project and the 100MW Nur Navoi solar project.

"Our relationship with power is a very old one," said Ilholm Jon Teshabaev, General Director of Uzbekistan Masdar. "We know our history, our old industries and how we generated power. But the world is moving forward and so have we."

Masdar and the Government of Uzbekistan are advancing the country's first standalone Battery Energy Storage System, or BESS, according to the documentary. The system aims to balance the grid, stabilize supply and ensure reliable clean power around the clock.

"From the beginning, our mission was to identify how Masdar could best support Uzbekistan's transition to a green and more sustainable energy system," said Abdulla Zayed, Director of Development and Investment at Masdar.

The Power to Change film series documents Masdar's renewable energy projects across multiple countries. The Uzbekistan episode showcases how the company is helping transition the country from its industrial past toward cleaner energy production through partnerships and innovation.

Masdar, the Abu Dhabi-based renewable energy company, has established itself as a major investor in Central Asia's clean energy transition through these landmark solar and wind developments.

title: Zayed Sustainability Prize
content:
Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026, held under the patronage of UAE President His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, concluded with over 60,000 attendees and 15 heads of state in attendance.

The five-day event, held January 11-15 under the theme 'The Nexus of Next: All Systems Go,' drew more than 400 ministers and government officials, double the number from the previous year. Hosted by Masdar, the week featured over 240 dedicated sessions with more than 800 speakers addressing sustainability challenges across energy, finance, food, water and nature.

The 2026 edition attracted 20% more attendees than last year's event, strengthening Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week's position as the world's largest sustainability gathering. The event highlighted the UAE's clean energy leadership and brought together business and civil society leaders to advance dialogue on key sustainability issues.

Participants addressed global energy systems transformation, food security, nature preservation and water security during the week-long convergence in the capital. The gathering underscored renewed commitments to accelerate real-world solutions and drive sustainable progress across interconnected global systems.

title: Acwa Recognized as a Global Leader in Water at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid
content:
Acwa won both Desalination Company of the Year and Desalination Plant of the Year at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, the company said on June 3. The Saudi Arabia-based firm operates 9.7 million m³/day of water desalination capacity, making it the world's largest private water desalination company.

The Global Water Awards, organized by Global Water Intelligence since 2006, recognize excellence across 15 categories in the international water sector. Acwa's Rabigh 3 Independent Water Project, a 600,000 m³/day seawater reverse osmosis plant serving the Makkah region, achieved availability levels above 99%, the company said. The facility recently completed a refinancing aligned with IFC Blue Finance Guidelines, marking the company's first such alignment.

Dr. Tariq Nada, head of Technical Services, Global Water at Acwa, said the dual award recognized "the engineers, operators and partners across our entire ecosystem." He added, "We are proud to provide a significant share of Saudi Arabia's desalinated water capacity."

Yasir Mahmoud, executive vice president Global Water at Acwa, said: "Being named Desalination Company of the Year, alongside Desalination Plant of the Year, is the clearest signal yet that our model works — lower energy intensity, higher availability and smarter financing."

The company operates 109 assets across operation, advanced development, and construction stages. Acwa has positioned itself as both a leader in water desalination and a participant in the energy transition, with investments in green hydrogen at scale.

title: Acwa, BCDA Advance Philippines' Clean Energy Transition with 500-MW Solar-Plus-Storage Project in New Clark City
content:
Acwa Power Philippines signed a contract to develop a 500-MW solar and battery storage facility across 500 hectares in New Clark City, the Philippines' flagship smart city development.

The integrated solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage system represents an initial investment of at least USD 400,000 per megawatt, with capacity reaching up to 500 MW upon full build-out, subject to regulatory approvals and expansion phases. Acwa Power Philippines, a subsidiary of Saudi-listed Acwa Power Company, signed the contract of lease with the Bases Conversion and Development Authority (BCDA).

The project will power data centers and advanced industries within New Clark City. Finance Secretary Frederick D. Go said the investment "lays the foundation upon which semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, and AI-driven industries can be built at utility scale." He added it strengthens New Clark City as a regional investment destination while creating jobs.

The development aligns with Philippine President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr.'s administration goals for sustainable economic growth and energy security.

Acwa Power Chief Executive Officer Dr. Samir J Serhan said the partnership reflects investor confidence in the Philippines for large-scale clean infrastructure. "Working alongside BCDA gives this initiative both strategic clarity and long-term momentum. We see an opportunity to help power industrial growth while lowering energy costs and supporting the country's broader economic ambitions through reliable renewable energy," he said.

Acwa Power Company operates desalination plants and renewable energy projects globally and is positioned as a leader in energy transition and green hydrogen development.

title: Acwa and Emerging Power Inc. Sign Joint Development Agreement (JDA) to Build up to 5,000 MW Clean Energy Platform by 2030 in the Philippines
content:
Acwa Power Philippines and Emerging Power Inc. signed a Joint Development Agreement to jointly pursue up to 5,000 MW of renewable energy and gas-fired generation capacity across the Philippines by 2030.

Under the agreement signed on June 2, 2026, the partners will identify and co-develop new power projects nationwide. A near-term priority is joint participation in the Philippines' Green Energy Auction Program, where they intend to submit competitive proposals for renewable energy investments.

Emerging Power Inc. is the clean energy arm of Nickel Asia Corporation, one of Southeast Asia's largest lateritic nickel ore producers. Acwa Power is a Saudi Arabia-based company and the world's largest private water desalination company.

"The Philippines has set one of the most ambitious clean energy trajectories in Southeast Asia, and reaching it requires partners who can move at scale and at pace," said Dr. Samir J Serhan, Acwa's Chief Executive Officer. "We bring three decades of delivering large-scale power and water assets on time and on budget."

Martin Antonio G. Zamora, Chairman and CEO of EPI, said the collaboration "actively accelerates the nation's transition to a low-carbon economy" and aims to build "scalable, reliable, and sustainable power infrastructure that will energize Filipino communities."

Salman M. Baray, Acwa's Country General Manager in the Philippines, said the agreement represents "the collaboration we've been building toward since we established our presence in the Philippines last year." He described the Green Energy Auction Program as the initial test case, with a longer pipeline of projects to follow.
